sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current player position more carefully
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
player_cells = []
for r in range(28, 36):
    for c in range(17, 26):
        ch = ascii_lines[r][c]
        if ch in ('W', 'w'):
            player_cells.append((r, c, ch))
print(f"Player cells: {player_cells}")

# Check target position
target_cells = []
for r in range(28, 36):
    for c in range(17, 26):
        ch = ascii_lines[r][c]
        if ch in ('O', 'b'):
            target_cells.append((r, c, ch))
print(f"Target cells near player: {target_cells}")

# Try moving RIGHT to see if target enters corridor
result = action(['RIGHT'])
print(f"\nRIGHT result: done={result.get('done')}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")

# Check positions after RIGHT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('O', 'b', 'W', 'w'):
        b = n['boundary']
        if len(b) >= 2:
            rows = [p[0] for p in b]
            cols = [p[1] for p in b]
            print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- Movement: 5 rows per UP/DOWN, ~4-5 cols per LEFT/RIGHT
- Blue L-shape at bottom-left changed shape (cols 7-8 → 3-4), suggesting actions affect multiple objects
- Need to reconsider goal: maybe target needs to reach a specific zone, not just overlap player
- The corridor (cols 24-33) might be the target zone
- Plan: Try RIGHT to move target into corridor area

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current player position more carefully
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
player_cells = []
for r in range(28, 36):
    for c in range(17, 26):
        ch = ascii_lines[r][c]
        if ch in ('W', 'w'):
            player_cells.append((r, c, ch))
print(f"Player cells: {player_cells}")

# Check target position
target_cells = []
for r in range(28, 36):
    for c in range(17, 26):
        ch = ascii_lines[r][c]
        if ch in ('O', 'b'):
            target_cells.append((r, c, ch))
print(f"Target cells near player: {target_cells}")

# Try moving RIGHT to see if target enters corridor
result = action(['RIGHT'])
print(f"\nRIGHT result: done={result.get('done')}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")

# Check positions after RIGHT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('O', 'b', 'W', 'w'):
        b = n['boundary']
        if len(b) >= 2:
            rows = [p[0] for p in b]
            cols = [p[1] for p in b]
            print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
